text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Raymond Kowalski
Medical Record Number: [ON FILE]
Accession Number: SP-[REDACTED]
Ordering Physician: Surgical Oncology / Colorectal Surgery
Clinical Service: Surgical Oncology
Procedure: Laparoscopic Sigmoid Colectomy with Primary Anastomosis
Clinical Indication: Sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma, known KRAS G12D-mutant, Stage IV; resection for local tumor control following neoadjuvant/induction systemic chemotherapy (mFOLFOX6 + bevacizumab x 12 cycles followed by LV5FU2 + bevacizumab maintenance, subsequently FOLFIRI + ramucirumab)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FINAL DIAGNOSIS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A. SIGMOID COLON, LAPAROSCOPIC RESECTION:

   1. MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED ADENOCARCINOMA, ypT3
      - Post-treatment histologic tumor regression grade: Grade 2 (moderate response)
        per Ryan/AJCC criteria (>10% but <50% viable tumor cells remaining; residual
        tumor accompanied by fibrosis and treatment effect)
      - Tumor extends through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal (pericolic)
        adipose tissue (ypT3)
      - No penetration of the visceral peritoneum; no involvement of adjacent
        organs or structures (ypT3 confirmed)
      - Greatest dimension of residual viable tumor: 1.9 cm (within a total treatment
        bed/fibrotic scar of approximately 2.7 cm)
      - Histologic grade (of residual viable component): Moderately differentiated
        (grade 2/3)

   2. LYMPH NODE STATUS: 2 OF 13 REGIONAL LYMPH NODES POSITIVE FOR METASTATIC
      ADENOCARCINOMA (ypN1b)
      - Positive nodes: 2 pericolorectal lymph nodes with metastatic deposits
        measuring 0.4 cm and 0.6 cm in greatest dimension
      - Extranodal extension: Not identified
      - Remaining 11 lymph nodes: negative for malignancy (0/11)
      - Treatment effect in lymph node deposits: Present (focal fibrosis, mucin pools
        without viable epithelium in 1 of 13 nodes; acellular mucin pools not counted
        as viable metastasis per AJCC 8th edition guidelines)

   3. RESECTION MARGINS:
      - Proximal resection margin: NEGATIVE for carcinoma (uninvolved; >5 cm from
        nearest tumor)
      - Distal resection margin: NEGATIVE for carcinoma (uninvolved; >4 cm from
        nearest tumor)
      - Circumferential (radial/mesorectal) resection margin (CRM): NEGATIVE
        (closest distance of invasive tumor to inked CRM: 2.1 mm; margin is
        considered negative at >1 mm per CAP/AJCC guidelines)

   4. LYMPHOVASCULAR INVASION: PRESENT (focal lymphovascular invasion identified
      in pericolorectal adipose tissue adjacent to the treatment bed; D2-40
      immunostain confirms lymphatic involvement)

   5. PERINEURAL INVASION: PRESENT (identified at the deep invasive front of residual
      viable tumor)

   6. TUMOR DEPOSITS (pericolic): Not identified as separate tumor deposits distinct
      from nodal disease (N1b classification applies)

   7. TUMOR PERFORATION: Not identified

   8. TREATMENT EFFECT: Present and prominent. Extensive hyalinized fibrosis,
      acellular mucin pools, hemosiderin-laden macrophages, and chronic inflammatory
      infiltrate replacing areas of prior tumor are identified throughout the treatment
      bed, consistent with prior systemic chemotherapy effect. Viable tumor nests
      represent a minority of the overall treatment bed area.

B. OMENTUM (submitted separately, labeled "pericolic fat"): NEGATIVE FOR
   MALIGNANCY. Fibrofatty tissue with no carcinoma identified.

C. DONUT RINGS (proximal and distal staple line margins): NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PATHOLOGIC STAGING (AJCC 8th Edition, post-treatment)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   Primary Tumor (yp):    ypT3
   Regional Lymph Nodes:  ypN1b (2/13 positive)
   Distant Metastasis:    M1b (synchronous hepatic and pulmonary metastases,
                               per clinical/radiographic staging; not submitted
                               for surgical pathology in this specimen)
   Overall Stage Group:   ypT3 ypN1b M1b = Stage IVB

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GROSS DESCRIPTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Specimen A: Received fresh, then placed in formalin, labeled "sigmoid colon resection" is a segment of large intestine measuring 22.0 cm in length and up to 4.2 cm in circumference when opened. The serosa is smooth and glistening over most of the specimen, with a focal area of fibrotic puckering and desmoplastic reaction over a 3.5 x 3.0 cm area on the antimesenteric surface corresponding to the tumor site. The attached mesenteric/pericolorectal adipose tissue is unremarkable grossly.

On opening via longitudinal incision, a firm, indurated, pale-gray to white circumferential/semicircumferential mural thickening and scar-like lesion is identified in the sigmoid colon, measuring 2.7 x 2.2 x 1.0 cm. The overlying mucosa shows flattening and effacement without a discrete polypoid or exophytic component, consistent with a predominantly treated/regressed tumor mass. The cut surface of the lesion is gray-white, firm, and fibrotic with only subtle areas of residual tan-yellow soft tissue possibly representing viable tumor. No obvious areas of necrosis or frank mucosal ulceration are identified at gross examination.

The proximal resection margin is represented by a surgical staple line and is located 8.0 cm from the nearest edge of the tumor. The distal resection margin is located 6.5 cm from the nearest edge of the tumor. The circumferential radial margin (CRM) at the deepest aspect of invasion is grossly estimated at 0.3 cm from the serosal/adventitial surface.

Proximal and distal donut rings (anastomotic staple line rings) are received separately, tan-pink mucosal rings with no gross lesions, submitted in total.

The attached pericolorectal adipose tissue is serially sectioned revealing multiple lymph node candidates. A total of 13 lymph node candidates are submitted. Two enlarged, firm lymph nodes measuring 0.6 cm and 0.4 cm are identified. The remaining lymph nodes are 0.3 cm or less. No gross extranodal extension is seen.

Representative sections from the tumor bed, perpendicular to the CRM, are entirely submitted (8 cassettes) along with sections from both resection margins.

Specimen B: Received in formalin, labeled "pericolic fat," is a tan-yellow irregular fragment of fibrofatty tissue measuring 4.5 x 3.0 x 1.2 cm. Serial sectioning shows homogeneous yellow fat with no discrete lesions or lymph node candidates identified. Submitted entirely in 3 cassettes.

Specimen C: Received in formalin, labeled "proximal and distal donuts," are two circular staple-line ring specimens, each measuring approximately 2.8 cm in maximum dimension. No lesions are identified. Submitted entirely in 2 cassettes.

Total cassettes: 13

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Sections from the tumor bed demonstrate a 2.7 cm treatment bed with extensive hyalinized fibrosis, acellular mucin pools (some with associated foreign body giant cell reaction), hemosiderin-laden macrophages, and a background of chronic lymphocytic and plasmacytic inflammatory infiltrate. These features are consistent with treatment response to prior systemic chemotherapy (fluoropyrimidine/oxaliplatin/irinotecan-based regimens and anti-angiogenic agents).

Residual viable adenocarcinoma is identified as irregular, moderately differentiated glandular structures (tubular and cribriform architecture) with nuclear pseudostratification, luminal dirty necrosis, nuclear enlargement, and prominent nucleoli. Viable tumor cells are scattered irregularly throughout the fibrotic treatment bed and focally at the invasive front extending into the pericolorectal adipose tissue. Tumor budding is identified focally (Bd2 per ITBCC 2016 criteria: 5-9 buds per 0.785 mm2 hot spot). Overall, residual viable tumor comprises approximately 30-35% of the total treatment bed area, consistent with Ryan/AJCC Tumor Regression Grade 2 (moderate response: >10% but <50% viable tumor remains).

The tumor penetrates through the full thickness of the muscularis propria and extends into the pericolorectal adipose tissue. The visceral peritoneum (serosa) is not breached; the closest serosal approach by tumor is approximately 3.5 mm. Depth of invasion beyond the muscularis propria is up to 8 mm into pericolorectal fat.

Lymphovascular invasion is confirmed: D2-40 immunostain highlights dilated lymphatic channels containing intraluminal tumor cell clusters in the pericolorectal adipose tissue adjacent to the treatment bed. Small vessel (vascular) invasion by tumor is not definitively identified on H&E or D2-40/CD31.

Perineural invasion is identified at the deep invasive front; a large-caliber nerve bundle measuring approximately 0.4 mm in diameter shows near-circumferential tumor infiltration.

Two of thirteen (2/13) pericolorectal lymph nodes are positive for metastatic adenocarcinoma (see above). One additional lymph node (included in the 11 negative) shows acellular mucin pools with fibrosis but no viable tumor cells, interpreted as treated metastatic disease with complete histologic response in that node; this node is classified as ypN0 per AJCC 8th edition convention (acellular mucin pools following treatment are not counted as viable metastasis).

Proximal and distal resection margins are negative for carcinoma. The circumferential radial margin is negative (tumor is 2.1 mm from the inked margin at the closest point).

The non-neoplastic sigmoid colon mucosa shows mild chronic colitis/architectural distortion, likely related to prior partial obstruction and ischemic changes. No dysplasia is identified in the background mucosa. No adenomatous polyps are seen in this specimen.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ANCILLARY STUDIES
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on sections of residual viable tumor):
- CDX2: Diffusely and strongly positive (3+) in residual tumor nuclei
- CK20: Diffusely positive (2-3+) in residual tumor cells
- CK7: Negative
- D2-40 (podoplanin): Positive in lymphovascular invasion foci (see above)
- CD31: Highlights vascular structures; no definitive intravascular tumor emboli
- MLH1: Retained nuclear expression (intact)
- MSH2: Retained nuclear expression (intact)
- MSH6: Retained nuclear expression (intact)
- PMS2: Retained nuclear expression (intact)
  Interpretation: Mismatch repair PROFICIENT (pMMR). No loss of MMR protein
  expression after chemotherapy treatment. Consistent with microsatellite stable (MSS)
  status as previously determined on the diagnostic biopsy specimen.
- Ki-67 (MIB-1): Elevated proliferation index in residual viable tumor (approximately
  55-60% of tumor cell nuclei positive in hot spots)

NOTE: KRAS mutation testing was previously performed on the diagnostic biopsy specimen
(KRAS p.G12D, codon 12, exon 2; VAF 42.3%) and on ctDNA at progression (VAF 3.8%).
Repeat molecular testing on this resection specimen is deferred to treating clinicians;
blocky FFPE material from this resection is available for future molecular studies upon
request. No additional molecular or FISH studies are reflexed on this specimen at this
time per institutional protocol for previously molecularly characterized tumors.

[PENDING]: Synoptic CAP colorectal cancer protocol checklist elements to be
finalized upon completion of all ancillary stain interpretation — report to be
updated if any preliminary result requires amendment. No amendments are anticipated
based on current findings.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
COMMENT
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

This sigmoid colon resection demonstrates a moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma
(ypT3 ypN1b) with Grade 2 histologic tumor regression (partial response per Ryan
criteria / AJCC 8th edition; CAP protocol Tumor Regression Score 2) following
prolonged neoadjuvant/induction systemic chemotherapy. The degree of treatment-induced
fibrosis and acellular mucin pools throughout the tumor bed indicate a meaningful
but incomplete pathologic response, consistent with the radiographic partial response
documented on serial cross-sectional imaging.

The presence of residual regional nodal metastases (2/13), lymphovascular invasion,
and perineural invasion in the setting of known distant metastatic disease (hepatic
and pulmonary) confirms ongoing Stage IVB disease. The negative circumferential
resection margin (2.1 mm clearance) and negative longitudinal margins are favorable
from a local control standpoint.

The retained expression of all four mismatch repair proteins (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2)
in the residual tumor is concordant with the pMMR/MSS status established at initial
diagnosis. This finding is consistent with the expected biological behavior of this
tumor and precludes benefit from immune checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy targeting
PD-1/PD-L1.

Correlation with prior molecular profiling (KRAS G12D, TP53 p.R248W, APC p.Q1367*,
SMAD4 p.R361H, PIK3CA p.E545K; NGS performed on diagnostic biopsy specimen) and
clinical history is recommended.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SIGNING PATHOLOGIST: Attending Surgical Pathologist, MD
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
[Signature block on file]
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
